name: Bédar Mining Loop
name_original: Llanos del Garrapancho — circular desde Bédar
description: This moderate 9.6-mile touring cycling route from Bédar offers panoramic views, olive groves, and historic mining routes.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Bédar offers a diverse landscape characterized by arid hills, historical mining areas, and proximity to coastal villages. The region features a mix of challenging climbs through mountainous terrain and more moderate routes exploring rural paths and salt flats. This area provides varied elevations and scenic vistas, making it suitable for different levels of touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Bédar The most popular touring cycling route is Bédar Mining Loop, a 9.6 miles (15.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 16 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the historical mining heritage of the Bédar area. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Mojácar Seafront Bike Path – Mojácar Village loop from Turre, a moderate 18.1 miles (29.2 km) path. This route combines coastal views with ascents into the charming Mojácar village. Local touring cyclists also love the Carros Salt Flat – Mojácar Village loop from Los Tres Amigos, a 46.6 miles (75.0 km) trail leading through salt flats and hilly village landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 50 minutes. Touring cycling around Bédar is defined by historical mining trails, coastal paths, and hilly village routes. The network offers options for both moderate and advanced touring cyclists. The routes in Bédar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Bédar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Bédar?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Bédar, offering a variety of experiences from moderate to difficult. The komoot community has explored these routes over 200 times.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Bédar?
A: Bédar's touring cycling routes are primarily moderate to difficult. There are 6 moderate routes and 16 difficult routes, catering to cyclists looking for a good challenge with significant climbs and varied terrain.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Bédar?
A: Yes, many of the routes in Bédar are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046171622/bedar-mining-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bédar Mining Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34347500" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carros Salt Flat – Mojácar Village loop from Los Tres Amigos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the typical distances for touring cycling routes in Bédar?
A: Route distances vary significantly. Moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046171622/bedar-mining-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bédar Mining Loop&lt;/a&gt; are around 15 km (9.6 miles), while more challenging routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34347500" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carros Salt Flat – Mojácar Village loop from Los Tres Amigos&lt;/a&gt; can extend to 75 km (46.6 miles).

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Bédar?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Bédar is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder and more comfortable for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, sometimes wetter, winter months.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Bédar?
A: While Bédar's routes tend to be moderate to difficult, families with older children or those comfortable with some climbs might enjoy shorter, moderate options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046171622/bedar-mining-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bédar Mining Loop&lt;/a&gt;, at 15.4 km, offers a manageable distance with historical interest.

Q: What can I see along the touring cycling routes in Bédar?
A: Many routes offer scenic views and pass by interesting landmarks. You might encounter historical mining areas, coastal vistas, and charming villages. Notable attractions in the wider area include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5060861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mojácar Old Town&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5246581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Aguas in Paraje Natural Karst en Yesos de Sorbas&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2831149" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Statue of the Sacred Heart of Jesus on Cerro del Espíritu Santo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Which touring cycling routes are suitable for experienced cyclists looking for a challenge?
A: Experienced cyclists will find plenty of challenging options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38289638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bédar Village – Fuente del Campico loop from Los Gallardos&lt;/a&gt; covers 53.2 km with over 1000m of elevation gain, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19353406" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Gallardos Mining Trail – Bédar Village loop from Turre&lt;/a&gt; offers 43.2 km of demanding terrain.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass through or near villages like Mojácar, Turre, and Los Gallardos, where you can find cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel. Planning your route to include these stops is recommended, especially on longer rides.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Bédar?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscape, which combines arid hills, historical mining areas, and coastal views, offering a unique and varied cycling experience.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that offer coastal views?
A: Yes, routes like the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38263519" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mojácar Seafront Bike Path – Mojácar Village loop from Turre&lt;/a&gt; combine coastal paths with ascents into charming villages, providing beautiful sea views.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Bédar?
A: Bédar's terrain is diverse, featuring arid hills, historical mining trails, and rural paths. You can expect a mix of paved roads and unpaved sections, with significant elevation changes, especially on routes venturing into the mountainous areas.
tips: If you wait a little and take a break, a herd of goats might come by. Everything smells of thyme and herbs

Starting at the car park below Bedar swimming pool local (piscina) head up the short hill towards Serena then on to Los Pinos, climbing then turning right off the road, on to a track towards Paco de los Albaricos.
The top is a view towards Lubrin, and in the winter snow can be seen on the Sierra Nevada Granada.
Down towards the fuente, where goats drink from, and to the bar la Montana, where subject to opening times a beer can be bought.
Turning right on to the main road back to Bedar, great sweeping views and bends descending all the time.
Two bars either side of the road, turn right into the town to see the church and a monument of a miner, with great views towards Mojacar and Los Gallardos. 
The best thing about this ride all the work is done at the the first half, then it’s all free wheel back to Bedar start line ie the car park below the pool.
Beautiful, original Spanish mountain village with great vantage points, narrow streets, white houses and friendly people.

faqs.question: Where does this cycling route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The Llanos del Garrapancho circular route typically starts and ends in the village of Bédar. You can find parking within the village, making it convenient to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily a touring bicycle trail, featuring mostly paved surfaces. However, be prepared for some segments that might be unpaved, adding a varied experience to the ride through the mountainous terrain around Bédar.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as 'moderate', this route requires good fitness due to its elevation gain and some potentially unpaved sections. While not extremely challenging, beginners should have some cycling experience and be prepared for climbs.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several scenic points. Highlights include a View of Lucainena de las Torres and a panoramic View of the Sierra Nevada. The route also passes through Bédar Village itself, offering a glimpse into local life. Expect picturesque landscapes with olive groves, almond trees, and citrus orchards.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with a small section (4%) of the 'Ruta de la Minería de Bédar', which is known for its historical mining routes.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region around Bédar generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, providing comfortable temperatures and beautiful scenery without the intense heat of summer.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific restrictions against dogs on public cycling routes, it's generally recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in populated areas or near livestock. Consider your dog's fitness for cycling alongside you on a moderate route.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Llanos del Garrapancho circular route. It is a publicly accessible trail.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Bédar Village, where you can find local cafes and establishments for refreshments before or after your ride. Along the route itself, options may be limited, so it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect on this ride?
faqs.answer: Cyclists can enjoy panoramic views of the Mediterranean coast and lush green valleys. The landscape is dotted with traditional agricultural features like olive groves, almond trees, and citrus orchards, providing a picturesque backdrop throughout your journey.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: As indicated by its name, 'circular desde Bédar', this is a loop trail. You will start and finish your ride in the same location, Bédar.

waypoints.name: View of Lucainena de las Torres
